title: Conjecture 1.5
statement_text: For all $r,t\in\mathbb{N}$, there exists $c=c(r,t)\in\mathbb{N}$ such that for every graph $G$, if $G$ does not contain $W_{t\times t}$ or $K_{t,t}$ as an induced minor, and every induced subgraph $H$ of $G$ that is an outerstring graph satisfies $\operatorname{tw}(H)\leq r$, then $\operatorname{tw}(G)\leq c$.
context_text: This conjecture is closely related to property (v) of the paper's construction — that every outerstring induced subgraph of the constructed graphs has treewidth bounded by an absolute constant. It was already refuted by the construction of Alecu, Bonnet, Villafana, and Trotignon [5], and the present paper provides a further counterexample.
paper_contribution: Provides a second, simpler counterexample to Conjecture 1.5 that additionally has arbitrarily large girth and does not contain all trees as induced subgraphs; the proof establishes a slightly stronger property than (v) — see Theorem 6.2.
notes: Attributed to a private communication (Trotignon, 2025); the conjecture is disproved (not proved) by this paper.
